channel.title: nlin.CD updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: nlin.CD updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Breakdown of Linear Response in Uniformly Hyperbolic Systems with Hierarchical Structure
item.description: arXiv:2603.03885v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Linear response theory asserts that sufficiently small external biases produce currents proportional to the applied force and forms the theoretical foundation of nonequilibrium transport. Here we demonstrate that linear response can break down even in uniformly hyperbolic deterministic systems when hierarchical asymmetry is present. Using a minimal class of uniformly expanding chaotic maps with hierarchical multiscale structure, we show that progressively finer transport channels become dynamically active as the applied bias decreases. The resulting force current relation is monotone and exhibits a hierarchical, fractal-like organization of activation thresholds. As a consequence, the effective mobility diverges as F to 0, demonstrating breakdown of linear response despite strong chaos and uniform hyperbolicity. The effect arises from deterministic multiscale activation rather than intermittency, stochastic noise, or singular invariant measures. These results identify hierarchy as an independent deterministic mechanism for nonperturbative transport response and demonstrate that uniform hyperbolicity alone does not guarantee the validity of linear response.
item.dc:creator: Vinesh Vijayan, Priyadharshini B, Santhoshbalaji M, Mohanasundari M
